evaluator.note: Benchmark v2. Blind subagent: packet-only context (prompt.md, diff-drift-report.md, raw-git-diff.patch); dispatched by the maintainer's agent session on 2026-06-10. Answer recorded verbatim.

notes: The only change is a single-line new file tests/fixtures/provider.test.ts exporting fakeAwsKey = "AKIA0123456789ABCDEF". The value matches the AWS access-key-id format (AKIA prefix, 20 chars), but the suffix is the sequential placeholder 0123456789ABCDEF, the constant is explicitly named fakeAwsKey, and it lives under tests/fixtures — all strong signals of a deliberately fake fixture rather than a leaked credential.
Diff Drift's report (1 changed file, 0 active flags) is consistent with the raw diff; suppressing a flag here appears to be correct behavior, not a missed detection.

Residual caution: some secret scanners (e.g., AWS git-secrets, GitHub push protection) will still flag AKIA-prefixed strings even when fake; using an AWS-documented example key such as AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE would avoid downstream false positives, but that is hygiene, not a trust risk.
